마가복음 5:1.content: They came to the other side of the sea, into the country of the Gadarenes.

마가복음 5:2.content: When he had come out of the boat, immediately a man with an unclean spirit met him out of the tombs.

마가복음 5:3.content: He lived in the tombs. Nobody could bind him any more, not even with chains,

마가복음 5:4.content: because he had been often bound with fetters and chains, and the chains had been torn apart by him, and the fetters broken in pieces. Nobody had the strength to tame him.

마가복음 5:5.content: Always, night and day, in the tombs and in the mountains, he was crying out, and cutting himself with stones.

마가복음 5:6.content: When he saw Jesus from afar, he ran and bowed down to him,

마가복음 5:7.content: and crying out with a loud voice, he said, “What have I to do with you, Jesus, you Son of the Most High God? I adjure you by God, don’t torment me.”

마가복음 5:8.content: For he said to him, “Come out of the man, you unclean spirit!”

마가복음 5:9.content: He asked him, “What is your name?” He said to him, “My name is Legion, for we are many.”

마가복음 5:10.content: He begged him much that he would not send them away out of the country.

마가복음 5:11.content: Now on the mountainside there was a great herd of pigs feeding.

마가복음 5:12.content: All the demons begged him, saying, “Send us into the pigs, that we may enter into them.”

마가복음 5:13.content: At once Jesus gave them permission. The unclean spirits came out and entered into the pigs. The herd of about two thousand rushed down the steep bank into the sea, and they were drowned in the sea.

마가복음 5:14.content: Those who fed them fled, and told it in the city and in the country. The people came to see what it was that had happened.

마가복음 5:15.content: They came to Jesus, and saw him who had been possessed by demons sitting, clothed, and in his right mind, even him who had the legion; and they were afraid.

마가복음 5:16.content: Those who saw it declared to them what happened to him who was possessed by demons, and about the pigs.

마가복음 5:17.content: They began to beg him to depart from their region.

마가복음 5:18.content: As he was entering into the boat, he who had been possessed by demons begged him that he might be with him.

마가복음 5:19.content: He didn’t allow him, but said to him, “Go to your house, to your friends, and tell them what great things the Lord has done for you, and how he had mercy on you.”

마가복음 5:20.content: He went his way, and began to proclaim in Decapolis how Jesus had done great things for him, and everyone marveled.

마가복음 5:21.content: When Jesus had crossed back over in the boat to the other side, a great multitude was gathered to him; and he was by the sea.

마가복음 5:22.content: Behold, one of the rulers of the synagogue, Jairus by name, came; and seeing him, he fell at his feet,

마가복음 5:23.content: and begged him much, saying, “My little daughter is at the point of death. Please come and lay your hands on her, that she may be made healthy, and live.”

마가복음 5:24.content: He went with him, and a great multitude followed him, and they pressed upon him on all sides.

마가복음 5:25.content: A certain woman, who had a discharge of blood for twelve years,

마가복음 5:26.content: and had suffered many things by many physicians, and had spent all that she had, and was no better, but rather grew worse,

마가복음 5:27.content: having heard the things concerning Jesus, came up behind him in the crowd, and touched his clothes.

마가복음 5:28.content: For she said, “If I just touch his clothes, I will be made well.”

마가복음 5:29.content: Immediately the flow of her blood was dried up, and she felt in her body that she was healed of her affliction.

마가복음 5:30.content: Immediately Jesus, perceiving in himself that the power had gone out from him, turned around in the crowd, and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”

마가복음 5:31.content: His disciples said to him, “You see the multitude pressing against you, and you say, ‘Who touched me?’ ”

마가복음 5:32.content: He looked around to see her who had done this thing.

마가복음 5:33.content: But the woman, fearing and trembling, knowing what had been done to her, came and fell down before him, and told him all the truth.

마가복음 5:34.content: He said to her, “Daughter, your faith has made you well. Go in peace, and be cured of your disease.”

마가복음 5:35.content: While he was still speaking, people came from the synagogue ruler’s house saying, “Your daughter is dead. Why bother the Teacher any more?”

마가복음 5:36.content: But Jesus, when he heard the message spoken, immediately said to the ruler of the synagogue, “Don’t be afraid, only believe.”

마가복음 5:37.content: He allowed no one to follow him, except Peter, James, and John the brother of James.

마가복음 5:38.content: He came to the synagogue ruler’s house, and he saw an uproar, weeping, and great wailing.

마가복음 5:39.content: When he had entered in, he said to them, “Why do you make an uproar and weep? The child is not dead, but is asleep.”

마가복음 5:40.content: They ridiculed him. But he, having put them all out, took the father of the child, her mother, and those who were with him, and went in where the child was lying.

마가복음 5:41.content: Taking the child by the hand, he said to her, “Talitha cumi!” which means, being interpreted, “Girl, I tell you, get up!”

마가복음 5:42.content: Immediately the girl rose up and walked, for she was twelve years old. They were amazed with great amazement.

마가복음 5:43.content: He strictly ordered them that no one should know this, and commanded that something should be given to her to eat.
